# pack-package: Visual Deep Dive

Concentrated diagrams for [.github/workflows/pack-package.yml](../workflows/pack-package.yml). Companion to [WORKFLOW_ARCHITECTURE.md](WORKFLOW_ARCHITECTURE.md).

This workflow is the regression-safety net. Every PR merged to `development` produces a packed tarball you can pull down and inspect before anything ships to npm. Minimum prose. Maximum diagrams.

- [1. The whole picture](#1-the-whole-picture)
- [2. Triggers](#2-triggers)

## 1. The whole picture

How [pack-package.yml](../workflows/pack-package.yml) fits into the merge lifecycle.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    classDef trig fill:#0b3954,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef gate fill:#7c2d12,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef job fill:#1e3a8a,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef ext fill:#064e3b,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef file fill:#374151,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef out fill:#581c87,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    subgraph T["Triggers"]
        t1["pull_request closed\nbase = development"]:::trig
        t2["workflow_dispatch\n(manual button)"]:::trig
    end

    subgraph A["pack-package.yml"]
        G["bypass gate\nhead_ref != bump-version-branch\nOR event = workflow_dispatch"]:::gate
        J["pack-package job\nubuntu-latest"]:::job
    end

    subgraph F["Files read/written"]
        f1["package.json\nbuild:package script"]:::file
        f2["src/index.ts\nentry"]:::file
        f3["dist/\nbuilt CJS + ESM + DTS"]:::file
        f4["llm-exe-&lt;version&gt;.tgz\nthe tarball"]:::file
    end

    subgraph X["External"]
        x1["actions/checkout@v4"]:::ext
        x2["actions/setup-node@v4\nNode 24.x"]:::ext
        x3["actions/cache@v4\n~/.npm + node_modules"]:::ext
        x4["actions/upload-artifact@v4"]:::ext
        x5["registry.npmjs.org\n(install only)"]:::ext
    end

    subgraph O["Outputs"]
        o1["artifact name: package\nfile: llm-exe-*.tgz\nretention: 30 days"]:::out
    end

    t1 --> G
    t2 --> G
    G -->|allowed| J
    G -.->|blocked: version bump merge| skip(["skip"])
    x1 --> J
    x2 --> J
    x3 --> J
    x5 --> J
    J --> f1
    f1 --> f3
    f3 --> f4
    f4 --> x4
    x4 --> o1
```

## 2. Triggers

Two entry points. One bypass condition.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef trig fill:#0e7490,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef gate fill:#7c2d12,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef out fill:#1f2937,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    start([event arrives])
    start --> ev{event_name?}

    ev -->|workflow_dispatch| disp[manual run\nalways proceeds]:::trig
    ev -->|pull_request| pr{types includes closed?}

    pr -->|no| stop0([ignored])
    pr -->|yes| base{base.ref == development?}
    base -->|no| stop1([ignored])
    base -->|yes| merged{pull_request.merged?}

    merged -->|false: closed without merging| run0[job still evaluated\nbut typically no-op work]:::trig
    merged -->|true| head{head.ref == bump-version-branch?}

    head -->|yes| skip([bypass: skip pack-package]):::gate
    head -->|no| proceed[(run pack-package job)]:::out

    disp --> proceed
    run0 --> head
```

The bypass exists because version-bump PRs (auto-generated by the release flow) merge a `package.json` version change without source changes. Packing again at that moment is redundant. The `workflow_dispatch` escape hatch always wins.

Source: [.github/workflows/pack-package.yml](../workflows/pack-package.yml) lines 3-11 (triggers), line 19 (bypass).

[Back to top](#navigate)

---

## 3. The one-job DAG

Single linear job. No matrix, no fan-out.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef job fill:#1e3a8a,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef step fill:#374151,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef gate fill:#7c2d12,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    start([Workflow event])
    start --> dec{bypass gate}:::gate
    dec -->|blocked| stop([end])
    dec -->|allowed| J

    subgraph J["Job: pack-package (ubuntu-latest)"]
        direction TB
        s1["actions/checkout@v4"]:::step
        s2["./.github/actions/setup-node\nNode 24.x"]:::step
        s3["./.github/actions/cache\n~/.npm + node_modules"]:::step
        s4["npm install"]:::step
        s5["npm run build:package"]:::step
        s6["npm pack"]:::step
        s7["ls -la"]:::step
        s8["find tgz, write PACKED_PACKAGE_PATH to GITHUB_ENV"]:::step
        s9["actions/upload-artifact@v4\nname: package, retention: 30d"]:::step
        s1 --> s2 --> s3 --> s4 --> s5 --> s6 --> s7 --> s8 --> s9
    end
```

Permissions are deliberately narrow: `id-token: write` and `contents: read`. No PR writes, no issue writes. This job reads code and emits a tarball. Nothing else.

[Back to top](#navigate)

---

## 4. Step-by-step lifecycle

One run from event to uploaded artifact.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant E as Event
    participant J as pack-package job
    participant CO as actions/checkout
    participant SN as setup-node (composite)
    participant CA as cache (composite)
    participant NPM as npm
    participant TS as tsup
    participant FS as runner FS
    participant UA as upload-artifact

    E->>J: PR closed on development OR manual dispatch
    Note over J: bypass gate evaluated on job.if
    J->>CO: checkout default ref
    CO-->>FS: working tree at merge commit
    J->>SN: setup-node@v4 (Node 24.x, npm registry)
    SN-->>J: node and npm on PATH
    J->>CA: restore ~/.npm and node_modules by hash(package.json)
    CA-->>FS: cache hit or miss (warm start either way)
    J->>NPM: npm install
    NPM-->>FS: node_modules populated
    J->>NPM: npm run build:package
    NPM->>TS: tsup src/index.ts (cjs, esm, dts, externals)
    TS-->>FS: dist/index.js + index.mjs + index.d.ts
    J->>NPM: npm pack
    NPM->>FS: read package.json files allowlist (dist)
    NPM-->>FS: llm-exe-(version).tgz at repo root
    J->>FS: ls -la (visibility only)
    J->>FS: find . -maxdepth 1 -name 'llm-exe-*.tgz'
    FS-->>J: path string
    J->>J: write PACKED_PACKAGE_PATH to GITHUB_ENV
    J->>UA: upload artifact name=package, path=$PACKED_PACKAGE_PATH
    UA-->>J: artifact stored, retention 30 days
```

Source: [.github/workflows/pack-package.yml](../workflows/pack-package.yml) lines 22-53.

[Back to top](#navigate)

## 5. The build:package flow

What `npm run build:package` actually does and why it differs from `build:ci`.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    classDef in fill:#0e7490,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef step fill:#1e3a8a,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef ext fill:#7c2d12,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef out fill:#064e3b,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    A["src/index.ts\n(entry)"]:::in
    A --> B["tsup\nformat cjs,esm\n--dts --clean"]:::step
    B --> C["--external list\n(deps that consumers install\non their own)"]:::ext
    C --> C1["jsonschema"]:::ext
    C --> C2["json-schema-to-ts"]:::ext
    C --> C3["exponential-backoff"]:::ext
    C --> C4["@aws-sdk/credential-providers"]:::ext
    C --> C5["@aws-crypto/sha256-js"]:::ext
    C --> C6["@smithy/protocol-http"]:::ext
    C --> C7["@smithy/signature-v4"]:::ext

    B --> O1["dist/index.js (CJS)"]:::out
    B --> O2["dist/index.mjs (ESM)"]:::out
    B --> O3["dist/index.d.ts (types)"]:::out

    O1 --> P["npm pack reads\npackage.json files = [dist]"]:::step
    O2 --> P
    O3 --> P
    P --> TGZ["llm-exe-(version).tgz"]:::out
```

Why externals matter: the AWS and smithy modules are large optional adapters. Bundling them would bloat the tarball and force every consumer to ship Bedrock support even if they only use OpenAI. Marking them external keeps the package thin and lets consumers install only what they need.

| Script | Where it runs | Difference |
|--------|---------------|------------|
| `build:ci` | tests workflow | No externals declared. Used for typechecking the bundle in CI. |
| `build:package` | pack-package workflow | Externals declared. Used to produce the publishable tarball. |

Source: [package.json](../../package.json) lines 44-46.

[Back to top](#navigate)

---

## 6. Output cascade

What this workflow produces and who eats it.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    classDef src fill:#1e3a8a,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef out fill:#581c87,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef cons fill:#064e3b,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef human fill:#7c2d12,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    PP["pack-package.yml\nrun completes"]:::src
    PP --> A1["artifact: package\nllm-exe-(version).tgz\nretention 30 days"]:::out

    A1 --> H1["maintainer downloads\nbefore release"]:::human
    A1 --> C1["test-package.yml\n(manual dispatch, optional)\ninstalls tarball in isolation"]:::cons
    A1 --> H2["smoke test in a fresh project\nnpm install ./llm-exe-(version).tgz"]:::human

    H1 --> R["compare against last release\nsanity-check bundle size and exports"]:::human
    H2 --> R
    C1 --> R

    R --> PUB["npm publish\n(separate manual flow)"]:::human
```

Why the artifact has a 30-day retention instead of the GitHub default 90: 30 days covers a normal release cadence comfortably, keeps storage tidy, and forces stale tarballs to expire rather than accumulating across hundreds of merges.

Why this is not auto-consumed downstream: publishing to npm requires deliberate human approval. The tarball is evidence, not action. The maintainer decides when to ship.

[Back to top](#navigate)

---

## 7. Failure modes

Where this workflow can break and what falls out.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef fail fill:#7c2d12,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef effect fill:#374151,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef fix fill:#064e3b,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    F1["Bypass mis-evaluated\n(head_ref check)"]:::fail
    F1 --> F1E["legitimate PR skipped\nor version bump packed"]:::effect
    F1E --> F1X["dispatch manually\nor adjust head ref naming"]:::fix

    F2["npm install fails"]:::fail
    F2 --> F2E["registry timeout or\nlockfile drift"]:::effect
    F2E --> F2X["re-run job\nor pin package.json"]:::fix

    F3["tsup build fails"]:::fail
    F3 --> F3E["type error or\nmissing import in src/index.ts"]:::effect
    F3E --> F3X["fix source, push to development\nnext PR merge retries"]:::fix

    F4["npm pack produces no tgz"]:::fail
    F4 --> F4E["files allowlist empty\nor name field broken"]:::effect
    F4E --> F4X["check package.json\nfiles array and name"]:::fix

    F5["find returns multiple tgz files"]:::fail
    F5 --> F5E["PACKED_PACKAGE_PATH gets newlines\nupload-artifact path resolution breaks"]:::effect
    F5E --> F5X["unlikely: only one tgz per pack\nbut clean dist before pack if seen"]:::fix

    F6["Cache restore corrupted"]:::fail
    F6 --> F6E["stale node_modules\nbuild uses wrong dependency versions"]:::effect
    F6E --> F6X["bump package.json to bust\nhashFiles cache key"]:::fix

    F7["upload-artifact 4xx"]:::fail
    F7 --> F7E["transient GitHub Actions storage issue"]:::effect
    F7E --> F7X["re-run job, no source change needed"]:::fix
```

Note that the cache action references `matrix.node-version` in its key (see [actions/cache/action.yml](../actions/cache/action.yml) line 10). This workflow has no matrix, so that part of the key is empty. Caching still works because the OS prefix and `hashFiles('**/package.json')` make the key unique enough. It is a minor wart, not a bug.

[Back to top](#navigate)

## 8. Quick reference card

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    classDef k fill:#1e3a8a,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    classDef v fill:#374151,color:#fff,stroke:#000

    K1["File"]:::k --- V1[".github/workflows/pack-package.yml"]:::v
    K2["Triggers"]:::k --- V2["PR closed on development + workflow_dispatch"]:::v
    K3["Bypass"]:::k --- V3["head_ref = bump-version-branch (unless dispatch)"]:::v
    K4["Permissions"]:::k --- V4["id-token: write, contents: read"]:::v
    K5["Runner"]:::k --- V5["ubuntu-latest"]:::v
    K6["Node"]:::k --- V6["24.x via setup-node composite"]:::v
    K7["Cache"]:::k --- V7["~/.npm + node_modules keyed by package.json hash"]:::v
    K8["Build script"]:::k --- V8["npm run build:package (tsup with externals)"]:::v
    K9["Pack command"]:::k --- V9["npm pack"]:::v
    K10["Artifact name"]:::k --- V10["package"]:::v
    K11["Artifact file"]:::k --- V11["llm-exe-(version).tgz"]:::v
    K12["Retention"]:::k --- V12["30 days"]:::v
    K13["Job count"]:::k --- V13["1"]:::v
    K14["Matrix"]:::k --- V14["none"]:::v
```
